2605.22971.title: Can AI Guess What You Know? Performance Comparison of Large Language Models for Human Domain Knowledge Estimation From Communication Logs
2605.22971.paper_summary: Employees often struggle to identify ``who knows what,'' leading to organizational productivity losses. We investigate whether Large Language Models (LLMs) can infer individual domain knowledge directly from long-term Slack logs. Analyzing 27,188 messages from 43 users, we evaluated seven models (including Gemini, Claude, and GPT families) by comparing their zero-shot estimates against self-reported skill ratings from 27 participants. Gemini 2.5 Flash achieved the lowest error (MAE 21.13%), while GPT models showed significantly larger discrepancies. Notably, estimation accuracy depended only weakly on message volume, indicating that more text alone does not guarantee better inference. These findings demonstrate the feasibility and current limits of automated expertise mapping, highlighting the need for privacy-preserving deployments and richer, structure-aware representations of human knowledge.
2605.22971.paper_summary_zh: 員工常常難以識別「誰知道什麼」，導致組織生產力損失。我們調查了大型語言模型（LLMs）是否能直接從長期的Slack日誌中推斷個體的領域知識。分析了43名用戶的27,188條消息，我們評估了七個模型（包括Gemini、Claude和GPT系列），通過將它們的零-shot估計與27名參與者的自我報告技能評級進行比較。Gemini 2.5 Flash實現了最低的誤差（MAE 21.13%），而GPT模型顯示出顯著更大的差異。值得注意的是，估計準確性僅與消息量有弱相關，這表明僅僅更多的文本並不保證更好的推斷。這些發現展示了自動專業知識映射的可行性和當前限制，突顯了對於保護隱私的部署和更豐富、結構感知的人類知識表示的需求。
2605.22971.author: Ko Watanabe et.al.
